name: Brex Corporate Card MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Brex — the corporate card and spend management platform designed for startups and tech companies. Enables AI agents to query Brex transactions, budgets, expenses, and spend analytics. Supports finance automation workflows for startups using Brex as their primary spend management solution.
use_cases: Querying Brex transactions and spend data from finance automation agents
Retrieving budget utilization and department spend from financial reporting agents
Monitoring expense policy compliance from finance governance agents
Generating spend summaries and analytics from business intelligence agents
not_for: Autonomous expense approval or card management (requires human oversight)
best_when: A startup or tech company using Brex wants AI agents to query spend data, monitor budgets, and generate financial reports — reducing manual finance operations.
avoid_when: You need autonomous card issuance or expense approval. FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate spend and card data requires strict human oversight for write operations.

auth.notes: Brex OAuth 2.0. Multiple token types (user, team tokens). Use read-only scopes for reporting agents. Team tokens for company-wide data access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Brex is free for businesses; API access included
pricing.notes: Brex corporate card platform is free. API access included. MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: Brex API: rate limits documented per endpoint in developer docs

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate card and spend data — strict access controls required
Brex has multiple token types (user, team) — use appropriate scope for agent use case
Card management and spend limit changes require human authorization
Community MCP implementation — verify against current Brex API documentation
Brex API versioning: check for breaking changes before upgrading
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Corporate spend and card data. SOC2 and PCI-DSS certified. OAuth with scope granularity. Write operations require human oversight.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate financial data. SOC2 and PCI-DSS certified. OAuth scopes well-defined. Card operations require human oversight.
